req.title: Patient portal login (negative)
req.statement: A patient shall log in using SSO; failed attempts shall be rate-limited and notify the patient. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers SSO-based patient login with rate-limiting on failed attempts and notifications. Test design targets: (1) EP – valid SSO assertion produces a session (positive); (2) NEG – invalid/denied SSO assertion is rejected without a session; (3) BVA – the exact rate-limit threshold (N attempts) triggers HTTP 429 and patient notification; (4) STC – a mid-session logout followed by re-login exercises the idle→active→logged-out→active state chain; (5) DT – missing mandatory SSO elements (no subject, no audience) are each independently rejected. This covers all three mandatory kinds (positive, negative, boundary) plus extended coverage of state transitions and decision-table paths.

tests.name: test_sso_login_valid_assertion_establishes_session
preconditions: A well-formed, unexpired SSO assertion is available for a known patient
No prior failed attempts exist for this patient
actions: POST /portal/login with a valid SSO assertion payload
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 200 or 302 redirect to patient dashboard
Response contains a session token or Set-Cookie header
Response body or redirect target does not contain an error message
trace: R-HLT-073: 'A patient shall log in using SSO'
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <fixture> import api_client, valid_sso_assertion, reset_rate_limit

def test_sso_login_valid_assertion_establishes_session(api_client, valid_sso_assertion, reset_rate_limit):
    reset_rate_limit(api_client)
    payload = {"sso_assertion": valid_sso_assertion}
    response = api_client.post("/portal/login", json=payload, allow_redirects=False)
    assert response.status_code in (200, 302), (
        f"Expected 200 or 302, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    has_session = (
        "session" in response.cookies
        or "Set-Cookie" in response.headers
        or "token" in response.json().get("data", {})
    )
    assert has_session, "No session token or cookie found in successful login response"

tests.name: test_sso_login_invalid_assertion_is_rejected
preconditions: An expired or tampered SSO assertion is available
No prior failed attempts exist for this patient identity
actions: POST /portal/login with an invalid SSO assertion (expired signature)
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error code indicating authentication failure
No session token or cookie is issued
trace: R-HLT-073: 'failed attempts shall be rate-limited and notify the patient'
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <fixture> import api_client, invalid_sso_assertion, reset_rate_limit

def test_sso_login_invalid_assertion_is_rejected(api_client, invalid_sso_assertion, reset_rate_limit):
    reset_rate_limit(api_client)
    payload = {"sso_assertion": invalid_sso_assertion}
    response = api_client.post("/portal/login", json=payload)
    assert response.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401 for invalid assertion, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body, "Response body must contain an error field"
    assert "session" not in response.cookies, "No session cookie must be set on auth failure"
    assert "Set-Cookie" not in response.headers or "session" not in response.headers.get("Set-Cookie", ""), \
        "Session must not be established on invalid SSO assertion"

tests.name: test_rate_limit_triggers_on_threshold_attempt
preconditions: Rate-limit threshold N is known (e.g. 5 attempts per window)
A deterministic invalid SSO assertion is available
Rate-limit counter for the test patient identity has been reset
actions: Reset the rate-limit counter for the target patient identity
POST /portal/login with an invalid assertion exactly N-1 times and assert each returns 401
POST /portal/login with an invalid assertion a Nth time
Capture status code and response body of the Nth attempt
expected: The first N-1 attempts each return HTTP 401
The Nth attempt returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
HTTP 429 response contains a Retry-After header
Patient notification event is emitted (verifiable via notification API or mock)
trace: R-HLT-073: 'Error pathways: 429 when too many attempts'
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <fixture> import api_client, invalid_sso_assertion, reset_rate_limit, get_rate_limit_threshold, assert_notification_sent

def test_rate_limit_triggers_on_threshold_attempt(api_client, invalid_sso_assertion, reset_rate_limit, get_rate_limit_threshold, assert_notification_sent):
    reset_rate_limit(api_client)
    threshold = get_rate_limit_threshold()
    payload = {"sso_assertion": invalid_sso_assertion}
    for attempt in range(1, threshold):
        r = api_client.post("/portal/login", json=payload)
        assert r.status_code == 401, f"Attempt {attempt}: expected 401, got {r.status_code}"
    final = api_client.post("/portal/login", json=payload)
    assert final.status_code == 429, (
        f"Expected 429 on attempt {threshold}, got {final.status_code}"
    )
    assert "Retry-After" in final.headers, "429 response must include Retry-After header"
    assert_notification_sent(patient_id=<fixture>.PATIENT_ID)

tests.name: test_sso_login_missing_mandatory_subject_element_rejected
preconditions: An SSO assertion with the mandatory subject element omitted is available
Rate-limit counter has been reset for this patient identity
actions: POST /portal/login with an SSO assertion missing the subject element
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body references missing or invalid mandatory element
trace: R-HLT-073: 'Negative paths including ... missing mandatory elements'
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <fixture> import api_client, sso_assertion_factory, reset_rate_limit

def test_sso_login_missing_mandatory_subject_element_rejected(api_client, sso_assertion_factory, reset_rate_limit):
    reset_rate_limit(api_client)
    assertion_no_subject = sso_assertion_factory(omit_fields=["subject"])
    payload = {"sso_assertion": assertion_no_subject}
    response = api_client.post("/portal/login", json=payload)
    assert response.status_code == 400, (
        f"Expected 400 for missing subject, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert any(
        kw in str(body).lower() for kw in ("subject", "mandatory", "missing", "invalid")
    ), f"Error body should reference missing element, got: {body}"
    assert "session" not in response.cookies, "No session must be set for malformed assertion"

tests.name: test_logout_then_relogin_restores_active_session
preconditions: A valid SSO assertion is available for a known patient
No prior failed attempts exist for this patient
actions: POST /portal/login with a valid SSO assertion to reach active state
POST /portal/logout with the session token to transition to logged-out state
POST /portal/login again with a fresh valid SSO assertion
expected: First login returns HTTP 200 or 302 with a session token
Logout returns HTTP 200 and invalidates the session
Second login returns HTTP 200 or 302 with a new, distinct session token
Original session token is no longer valid after logout
trace: R-HLT-073: 'conflicting state transitions must be tested'
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <fixture> import api_client, valid_sso_assertion, fresh_sso_assertion, reset_rate_limit

def test_logout_then_relogin_restores_active_session(api_client, valid_sso_assertion, fresh_sso_assertion, reset_rate_limit):
    reset_rate_limit(api_client)
    r1 = api_client.post("/portal/login", json={"sso_assertion": valid_sso_assertion}, allow_redirects=False)
    assert r1.status_code in (200, 302)
    session_token_1 = <helper_fn>.extract_session_token(r1)
    assert session_token_1, "First login must return a session token"
    r_logout = api_client.post("/portal/logout", headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {session_token_1}"})
    assert r_logout.status_code == 200, f"Logout failed: {r_logout.status_code}"
    r2 = api_client.post("/portal/login", json={"sso_assertion": fresh_sso_assertion}, allow_redirects=False)
    assert r2.status_code in (200, 302)
    session_token_2 = <helper_fn>.extract_session_token(r2)
    assert session_token_2, "Re-login must return a new session token"
    assert session_token_1 != session_token_2, "Re-login must issue a distinct session token"
    r_verify_old = api_client.get("/portal/me", headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {session_token_1}"})
    assert r_verify_old.status_code == 401, "Invalidated session token must be rejected"
